channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Police increase patrols after repeated motorbike vandalism at Canberra region sportsgrounds
item.description: Authorities in the Canberra region have stepped up police patrols following a series of vandalism incidents involving motorbikes and other small vehicles damaging local sportsgrounds. The latest incid...

item.title: Gauteng achieves 131% HIV prevention target with Lenacapavir rollout
item.description: The Gauteng health department has surpassed its first-month target for distributing Lenacapavir, a long-acting HIV prevention medication, with 6,130 eligible individuals initiated between June 8 and t...

item.title: NAB Seizes Bahria Icon Tower in Malik Riaz Anti-Money Laundering Case
item.description: The National Accountability Bureau (NAB) has taken physical possession of Karachi’s Bahria Icon Tower, valued at around Rs100 billion, as part of an ongoing investigation into property tycoon Malik Ri...

item.title: Educational Events and Reunions in Dindigul Colleges
item.description: The article highlights various educational initiatives and events across colleges in Dindigul and surrounding areas. Key activities include a national-level workshop organized by ANJAC and the Nationa...

item.title: Arusha Activist Mama C Embarks on Global Tour to Celebrate Black Panther Party's 60th Anniversary
item.description: Charlotte Hill O'Neal, known as Mama C, an Arusha-based activist and veteran of the Black Panther Party, is set to launch the 2026 UAACC Heal the Community Tour starting in Ecuador. The tour, which ha...

item.title: Amani G's Transformation and Musical Growth After Relocating to the US
item.description: Amani G, the Kenyan singer who first gained fame in 2018 for her powerful rendition of Alicia Keys' 'Girl on Fire', has captured the hearts of Kenyans again with her remarkable transformation after mo...

item.title: El Niño and Warming Oceans Pose Risks for California and Global Climate
item.description: The article details the escalating climate crisis driven by El Niño phenomena and rising ocean temperatures, highlighting their severe impacts on California and global ecosystems. Scientists warn that...

item.title: Rivers Group Urges Early Preparation for Students to Overcome Challenges

item.title: China and U.S. Compete for African Minerals via Rail Corridors
item.description: The article discusses the growing competition between China and the United States over control of African copper and cobalt resources, which are critical for clean energy technologies. Two major railw...

item.title: Rogers completes acquisition of MLSE stake with $4.35B deal
item.description: Rogers Communications Inc. has finalized a $4.35 billion deal to acquire the remaining 25% stake in Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment (MLSE) from Kilmer Sports Inc. This transaction marks a significan...

item.title: Tom Everett Scott Shares Behind-the-Scenes Stories from 'Elle' Prequel Set
item.description: The article explores Tom Everett Scott's experiences while working on the 'Elle' prequel to 'Legally Blonde' (2015). Scott, known for his role in the original film, discusses the challenges and creati...

item.title: B.C. Woman Embarks on 106-Kilometre Nonstop Swim Across Okanagan Lake
item.description: A Vancouver-based woman is set to attempt an unprecedented 106-kilometre nonstop swim across Okanagan Lake, a feat that would make her one of the few to achieve such a challenge. The swim, scheduled f...

item.title: Federal Judge Rejects Todd Blanche's Attempt to Close Epstein Files Lawsuit
item.description: U.S. District Judge Emmet Sullivan rejected Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche's motion to dismiss a lawsuit over the Epstein files, maintaining the case against him for allegedly violating the Epst...

item.title: Canberra prepares for the launch of popular frozen yoghurt brand Yo-Chi
item.description: The article discusses the excitement surrounding the upcoming launch of Yo-Chi, a self-serve frozen yoghurt brand, in Canberra. Despite the absence of specific dates or locations in the job ad, the br...

item.title: Kenya Power to Conduct Scheduled Power Outages in Five Counties
item.description: Kenya Power and Lighting Company (KPLC) has announced planned electricity outages across five counties on Tuesday, July 7, for essential maintenance work. The outages, which will last up to eight hour...

item.title: Tanzania strengthens economic ties through African Caucus engagement
item.description: Tanzania is intensifying preparations for the 2026 African Caucus Meeting to bolster continental economic partnerships and diversify international trade beyond traditional Western markets. Finance Min...

item.title: Full 2026 FIFA World Cup Fixtures and Match Details
item.description: The article provides an exhaustive breakdown of the 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule, highlighting the expanded 48-team format hosted jointly by Canada, the United States, and Mexico. It outlines the grou...

item.title: India's 2024 Budget Focuses on Economic Growth, Inflation Control, and Social Welfare
item.description: The 2024 Union Budget, presented by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, outlines a comprehensive economic strategy aimed at fostering growth, managing inflation, and enhancing social welfare. Key highlights...

item.title: Sigabo: Betchay at Mameng Collaborate on TV Interview Episode 11
item.description: The article discusses the latest episode of the TV show 'Sigabo,' where actors Betchay and Mameng engaged in a dramatic scene during their interview segment. The episode, titled 'Episode 11,' highligh...

item.title: Ross Kemp shares passionate reaction to England's World Cup win over Mexico and praises Jude Bellingham
